title: Solanum actinocalyx
taxonomy: Solanum actinocalyx is classified as a species within the Solanum genus of flowering plants. It is a member of the Solanaceae family, which includes other plants such as potatoes and tomatoes. Solanum actinocalyx is a part of the larger clade called Asterids, and specifically falls under the subclade Solanales. Further classification places it under the order of Ericales, and it is a part of the larger clade called Angiosperms. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among Solanum actinocalyx and its related plant species.

Its leaves and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help alleviate various health issues. intro: Solanum actinocalyx, also known as the Ecuadorian tree tomato or tree cherry, is a plant species belonging to the Solanaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Solanum actinocalyx contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
